qa_pairs.question: Within how many days are ROP payments made?
qa_pairs.answer: Within 60 days of the applicable date
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.4 ROP payments are made within 60 days of the applicable date.

qa_pairs.question: What is the issue date of this policy?
What company issued this policy?
What is the interest rate on overdue premiums in a reinstatement?
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.1 If the policy lapses, you may apply for reinstatement within 5 years if: You pay all past due premiums with interest at 6% per annum

qa_pairs.question: Who is the primary beneficiary?
Who are the contingent beneficiaries and their shares?
qa_pairs.answer: Sofia Isabella Martinez (Daughter) at 50% and Marco Daniel Martinez (Son) at 50%
qa_pairs.evidence: Contingent: Sofia Isabella Martinez (Daughter) - 50% / Marco Daniel Martinez (Son) - 50%

qa_pairs.question: Who is the insured?
What documents must beneficiaries submit to claim the death benefit?
qa_pairs.answer: A certified death certificate, a completed claim form, and government-issued ID.
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.3 Beneficiaries must submit: Certified death certificate / Completed claim form / Government-issued ID

qa_pairs.question: What is the only way to access accumulated value under this policy during the term?
qa_pairs.answer: Through full surrender (with the applicable ROP percentage) or by waiting until the end of the term for the full ROP payment.
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.3 The only way to access cash value is through: Full surrender (with applicable ROP percentage), OR / Waiting until end of term for full ROP payment

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the policy lapses due to non-payment within the grace period?
qa_pairs.answer: The policy lapses and the return of premium benefit is forfeited.
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.4 Grace period: 31 days. If premium is not paid within grace period, policy lapses and return of premium benefit is forfeited.

qa_pairs.question: What is the insurer's liability if the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of issue?
qa_pairs.answer: Only the premiums paid are returned without interest; no death benefit is payable.
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.1 SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid without interest. No death benefit is payable.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to the ROP benefit if the policy owner converts to permanent insurance?
qa_pairs.answer: The ROP benefit is forfeited and premiums paid to date are not returned.
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.4 Upon conversion: ROP benefit is forfeited / Premiums paid to date are NOT returned

qa_pairs.question: If a premium is paid late during the grace period, how is the ROP benefit affected?
qa_pairs.answer: The ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.5 Missed premiums cannot be made up. If a premium is paid late (during grace period), the ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.

qa_pairs.question: What percentage of premiums is returned if the policy is surrendered in Years 1-5?
What happens to the ROP benefit calculation upon reinstatement after a lapse?
qa_pairs.answer: The ROP benefit is recalculated to exclude the lapsed period.
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.2 Upon reinstatement, the ROP benefit is recalculated to exclude the lapsed period.

qa_pairs.question: Within how many years after lapse may the policy be reinstated?
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.1 If the policy lapses, you may apply for reinstatement within 5 years if...

qa_pairs.question: Are policy loans or partial withdrawals available under this policy?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Policy loans are not available and partial withdrawals are not permitted.
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.1 Policy loans are NOT available under this policy type. / 6.2 Partial withdrawals are NOT permitted.

qa_pairs.question: If the insured dies in Year 8, what total amount do the beneficiaries receive?
qa_pairs.answer: The full $300,000 death benefit plus 30% of the premiums paid to date (the Year 6-10 ROP rate), as illustrated in the policy example.
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.3 DEATH DURING TERM: If death occurs during the term, beneficiaries receive: Full death benefit ($300,000), PLUS a proportional premium refund based on the early surrender schedule. / Years 6-10: 30% of premiums paid returned

qa_pairs.question: If the insured survives to the end of the term but had one late payment during the grace period, will the full $32,400 be returned?
qa_pairs.answer: No. The full $32,400 end-of-term ROP requires all premiums to be paid on time. A late payment reduces the ROP benefit by 10%, so the return would be reduced.
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.3 CRITICAL: To receive the full return of premium at end of term, ALL premiums must be paid on time. / 4.5 If a premium is paid late (during grace period), the ROP benefit is reduced by 10% for each late payment.

qa_pairs.question: If the policy owner converts to permanent insurance after 10 years of paying premiums, do they receive any refund of the premiums paid?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Upon conversion the ROP benefit is forfeited and premiums paid to date are not returned. The conversion privilege requires giving up the ROP benefit entirely.
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.4 Upon conversion: ROP benefit is forfeited / Premiums paid to date are NOT returned

qa_pairs.question: Can the policy be contested after 2 years in force?
qa_pairs.answer: No. After 2 years the contestability period has passed. A new 2-year contestability period begins only upon reinstatement.
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.2 MATERIAL MISREPRESENTATION: We may contest the policy within 2 years of issue if there was material misrepresentation in the application. / 8.3 A new 2-year suicide and contestability period begins upon reinstatement.
